summary:
The glossiness of castings is primarily attributed to careful material selection and precise manufacturing techniques that prevent imperfections during processing. This ensures an aesthetically pleasing finish suitable for high-quality custom dentistry applications requiring accuracy and precision. To directly answer your query within two concise statements:

1) Proper use of dimensional-stable materials combined with meticulous control over every stage of fabrication minimizes defects responsible for rough surfaces; instead it promotes uniformity conducive to shiny edges seen clinically.
   
2) Effective technique application results in minimal disruption allowing natural luster development characteristic of professionally produced casts used extensively across various medical specialties demanding exact specifications like orthodontics prosthodontic implantology etc., thereby justifying why such features arise specifically under these conditions rather than other factors mentioned
